items.title: Enhanced Connector Status in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: The publishing console now displays connection statuses for various platforms, enhancing user experience by linking connection actions to the connector system. Key updates include the integration of a connector operator for status reading and improved UI elements for managing connections. This update sets the stage for future enhancements, including a live feed and task board.

items.title: Enhance Free Access for Dreamina Tasks in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the `/dreamina/tasks` endpoint to the `SKIP_AUTH_API_IDS`, allowing free access without authentication or billing. This change aligns the Dreamina service with other task endpoints, streamlining user experience. Key modifications were made in the settings file to implement this feature.

items.title: Introducing Dreamina: AI Talking-Photo Video Service in Backend 🎥
items.summary: This update integrates the Dreamina digital-human video service, enabling audio-driven talking photos using ByteDance's OmniHuman 1.5 technology. Key features include a cost-effective API for generating lip-synced videos from a portrait image and audio, along with comprehensive documentation in both English and Chinese. The implementation includes new endpoints, pricing structures, and successful test cases to ensure functionality.

items.title: Exciting New Video Service for OmniHuman in Platform Service 🤖
items.summary: This update introduces the 即梦 (Jimeng) video service to the volcengine worker, enabling the generation of lip-synced videos from portrait images and audio. Key changes include the implementation of V4 HMAC-SHA256 signing and the addition of a new API endpoint for video processing. The feature has been end-to-end proven with successful output, enhancing the capabilities of the Platform Service.

items.title: Enhance Task Processing with Optional Config in Publisher 🚀
items.summary: This update allows connector tasks to proceed without a mandatory PublishConfig, enhancing flexibility in task processing. The `process_task` function now handles cases where `task.config` is None, allowing for faster failure with clear guidance if no credentials are resolved. A total of 523 tests have passed, ensuring stability and reliability in the changes made.

items.title: Enhance Connector Publishing by Making Config ID Optional in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the requirement for a `config_id` in connector-backed publish tasks, facilitating a smoother transition to connector-driven publishing. The `PublishTaskCreateSerializer` has been modified to allow `config_id` to be optional, enabling tasks to be created without a `PublishConfig`. Additionally, internal document links are now rewritten using the task's `user_id` when no config is provided.

items.title: Enhance Autofill Sync for Registration and Reset Forms in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a latent bug affecting the registration and reset-password forms where autofilled values were not triggering the necessary input events. The `syncAutofilledValues()` function has been applied to the email and phone fields in both forms, ensuring that the correct values are submitted on the first click. All relevant files have been updated and the codebase is clean with no linting errors.

items.title: Fix login autofill issue for smoother access in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a user-reported issue where the first click on the login button is ignored when credentials are autofilled. By adding references to the input fields, the values are now synchronized with the form before validation, ensuring that the login request is sent on the first click. This fix applies to both email and username login components.

items.title: Enhance OAuth Credential Handling for Publishers in Publisher 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces OAuth support for the `linkedin` and `blogger` connectors, allowing them to fetch fresh bearer tokens from the AuthBackend. The changes include new functions for resolving OAuth tokens and adjustments to cookie handling, ensuring seamless integration with existing publisher workflows. A total of 20 connector tests were added, and all tests passed successfully.

items.title: Integrate PlatformStudio Submodule and Complete Phase-0 Spike in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update successfully integrates the PlatformStudio as a submodule, enhancing the article-to-video factory project. The Phase-0 spike has been moved to PlatformStudio, enabling it to drive the production renderer with real media, complete with burned-in captions. The plans directory now focuses on design and tracking, reflecting the latest developments in the project.

items.title: Burned-in Captions and Phase-0 Validation in Platform Studio 🎥
items.summary: This update integrates the Phase-0 spike into the rendering service, validating the renderer with real assets. Key changes include the addition of a burned-in caption renderer and enhancements to the FFmpeg build plan, allowing for seamless integration of captions in video outputs. The results demonstrate successful rendering of a 9:16 video with captions and voiceover, showcasing the capabilities of the updated pipeline.

items.title: Shrink empty-state magic-wand button for better UI in Nexior ✨
items.summary: This update reduces the size of the empty-state magic-wand button from 48px to 40px, enhancing the overall user interface. The icon size has also been adjusted accordingly, while the button's functionality remains unchanged, still allowing users to open the config drawer on mobile. These changes aim to improve the visual appeal and usability of the component.

items.title: Improve WeChat Login Error Handling in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a critical issue where an invalid WeChat code exchange resulted in a 500 error instead of a proper 401 response. The code now checks for the presence of 'access_token' and 'openid' in the response, logging any errors and raising a LoginFailed exception when they are absent. This change enhances the stability of the WeChat login process by providing clearer error messaging to clients.

items.title: Enhanced API Descriptions with Translations in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces translatable field descriptions for all 144 API services, converting hardcoded OpenAPI descriptions into dynamic references. The zh-cn source text is now embedded in each file's translations block, ensuring consistency and accuracy across languages. Additionally, 171 description corrections have been made to enhance clarity and correctness in API documentation.

items.title: Streamline Login Error Logging in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the login error handling in the AuthBackend to prevent expected login failures from being logged as ERROR tracebacks. By re-raising API exceptions without logging them, we reduce noise in the logs and ensure that only genuine server errors are flagged. This change maintains the same client response behavior while improving log clarity and reducing alert fatigue.

items.title: Update Android Build Settings in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: The default behavior for the `build-android` workflow has been updated to prevent automatic promotion to Production. Now, manual dispatches will default to the test track only, requiring explicit action to promote to Production. This change enhances control over release management for Android builds.

items.title: Revamp Service Detail Page with Global-First Hero and API Playground in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update transforms the service detail page into a global-first hero, featuring a primary recharge button that ensures users have a universal application. Additionally, an inline API playground has been introduced, allowing users to interact with API snippets directly on the page. Key components have been reused to enhance maintainability and reduce duplication across the codebase.

items.title: Update iOS Build Process to Manual Review in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: The default behavior for the `build-ios` dispatch has been changed to no longer auto-submit to Apple App Review. Now, it defaults to TestFlight-only unless explicitly set to auto-submit. This adjustment enhances control over the review process while keeping other distribution settings unchanged.

items.title: Optimize OpenAI Probes for Stability in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the E2E health probes for OpenAI chat and responses by implementing a curated model filter. The changes reduce false 500 errors and decrease probe traffic by five times, while maintaining customer-facing functionality. Key modifications include adjusting the probe model selection and increasing the test interval for better performance.

items.title: Streamline Credential Management by Removing Redundant Refresher in Publisher 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the Publisher's cookie-refresh subsystem, streamlining credential management as the AuthBackend now handles the credential lifecycle. Key files related to the refresher have been deleted, including multiple Python scripts and configuration files. The change enhances system reliability by providing clearer error messages for expired cookies, improving user experience.

items.title: Enhance Credential Management with Connector Vault in Publisher 🚀
items.summary: This update implements Phase 1 of the connector vault integration, prioritizing connector vault cookies over legacy configurations for improved credential management. Key changes include adjustments in `publish.py` to utilize the new `resolve_cookies` function and updates to the deployment configuration to include necessary environment variables. All tests have passed successfully, ensuring a smooth transition to this new system.

items.title: Unify Session Identity for Seamless Resuming in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update consolidates the session identity in the Coding Bridge, adopting the provider's real session ID for a unified experience. Key changes include improved session restoration, ensuring live sessions retain their state and functionality, such as the Stop button and typewriter effect. The redundant session fields have been collapsed into a single canonical ID, enhancing clarity and performance.

items.title: Streamlining Publisher with Connector Integration in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update outlines a staged development plan to transition PlatformPublisher's credential sourcing from its own configuration to the AuthBackend connector vault. Key phases include wiring in connector cookies, removing the existing cookie-refresh subsystem, and implementing end-to-end testing with real accounts. Documentation for each phase is provided to guide future implementation efforts.

items.title: Unify Session Identity for Seamless Resuming in Coding Bridge Agent 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves the issue of dual identities in coding sessions by adopting a single canonical identity based on the provider's real ID. The changes ensure that live sessions can now reattach to their historical counterparts without spawning duplicate clients. Key modifications include re-keying sessions and marking live transcripts, enhancing user experience and session continuity.

items.title: Enhance History Drawer with Brand Icons in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update replaces the colored provider text tags in the coding-bridge history drawer with the actual brand logos for Claude and Codex. The change improves readability and aligns with the existing design patterns by using SVG assets. Key modifications include the introduction of a new `providerIcon()` helper and updated styles for a more compact display.

items.title: Enhance API Dialog with Service Favicon in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update improves the **Try the API** dialog by displaying the selected service's favicon alongside its name in the dropdown. Utilizing Element Plus's `el-select` component, a new computed property retrieves the icon URL for the chosen service. This change enhances the user experience by providing a visual representation of the selected service.

items.title: Refine AI Chat Status Probe for Stability in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the AI Chat status probe to focus on a curated list of 11 stable production models, significantly reducing false outage signals. The probe's frequency has also been decreased from every 60 seconds to every 300 seconds, enhancing system performance. These changes aim to provide a more accurate representation of service availability and reduce unnecessary alerts.

items.title: Enhance API Translations with Dynamic Descriptions in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces translatable API field descriptions in the OpenAPI specification, allowing for dynamic localization. By integrating the existing translation pipeline, descriptions are now referenced via `$t()` keys, streamlining the process for multiple languages. The initial implementation focuses on the glm-chat-completions service, ensuring accurate translations are served based on user locale.

items.title: Optimize Checker for Efficiency in Platform Service 🤖
items.summary: This update reduces the Checker CronJob's probe interval from every 2 minutes to every 10 minutes, significantly lowering the number of probes. Additionally, the prompt for checking liveness has been simplified to a one-word response, drastically cutting token usage and costs associated with upstream services. These changes enhance performance while maintaining acceptable detection lag.

items.title: Improve BYOC Credential Error Handling in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the error handling for BYOC credentials by implementing a new function, `formatErrorDetail()`, which flattens nested error objects into a readable string. Previously, errors were not displayed, leading to confusion for users. Now, both extension and form submission errors will be clearly communicated, improving user experience.

items.title: Enhance cookie handling in Backend 🍪
items.summary: This update modifies the `ConnectorCredentialsSerializer` to accept list values for cookies in the BYOC credential payload. By changing the `payload` field to a `DictField`, both list-shaped and string-shaped payloads are now supported, ensuring proper validation and functionality for cookie-based connectors. This fix resolves the 400 error encountered during connection attempts.

items.title: Automatic i18n Updates from zh-CN in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces auto-generated internationalization (i18n) translation updates based on changes from the zh-CN source. Key modifications were made across multiple language files, enhancing the application's accessibility for users in various regions. The changes include new confirmation messages and error handling prompts in several languages.

items.title: Enhance Deployment Deletion with Force Option in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a force delete option for deployment-type applications when normal deletion fails. Users will now see a confirmation prompt asking if they want to force delete the application record, ensuring that stuck records can be removed. Key changes include modifications to the application operator and UI components to handle this new functionality.

items.title: Enhance deployment teardown with force option in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a `?force=true` parameter to the deployment teardown API, allowing the deletion of application records even when the underlying cloud resource fails to tear down. This feature provides operators with a solution for handling orphaned applications with corrupted metadata or unreachable resources. The change ensures that applications can be removed without leaving undeletable records in the system.

items.title: Revamped Landing Page with Enhanced Features in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces significant improvements to the landing page, including a new installation step guide for SDKs, enhanced code blocks using a unified `CodeSnippet` component, and visually appealing API category cards with icons. Additionally, the tab night mode has been fixed for better visibility, and all code snippets have been verified for accuracy against existing packages. The overall user experience is now more engaging and informative.

items.title: Boosted AI Models Count on Homepage in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: The homepage of UnifiedHub has been updated to reflect an increase in AI models from 200+ to 300+. This change involved modifications to the UnifiedHub component and localization files for both English and Chinese. The update ensures that users have the most accurate information regarding the current catalog size.

items.title: Add gear icon for Manage Keys button in Frontend Web ⚙️
items.summary: This update registers the `faGear` icon for the **管理密钥** (Manage Keys) button on the `/console/applications` page. Previously, this button rendered without an icon, while other buttons displayed theirs. The fix involved importing and adding the `faGear` icon to the central Font Awesome plugin, ensuring consistent iconography across the interface.

items.title: Enlarge Application ID Font Size for Better Readability in Frontend Web ✨
items.summary: This update increases the font size of the application ID displayed on the global balance card in the console from 11px to 13px. The change ensures consistency with surrounding text, enhancing overall readability for users. The modification was made in the ApiKeyQuickAccess component.

items.title: Removed cookie keep-alive feature in Backend 🚀
items.summary: The cookie keep-alive feature has been removed due to build issues with the Playwright image. This change eliminates several components including the Dockerfile, cronjob, and associated services, while ensuring that cookie connectors remain functional. The update streamlines the deployment process and maintains existing cookie READ functionality.

items.title: Enhance session settings restoration and editability in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update restores session settings such as model, working directory, reasoning effort, and edit mode when reloading a Coding Bridge conversation. Users can now edit these settings on every turn, ensuring a more flexible and consistent experience. The changes are complemented by server-side updates to persist these settings effectively.

items.title: Enhance Session Management in Coding Bridge Agent 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new session metadata management system that persists and restores per-session settings, including working directory, permission mode, and reasoning effort. Key files modified include `session_meta.py`, which now saves session data to a JSON file, and updates to `session.py` and `connection.py` to integrate this functionality. Overall, this enhancement improves the user experience by ensuring that sessions can resume with the correct context after a reload.

items.title: Enhance cookie image for seamless playwright installs in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the Dockerfile for the cookie keep-alive image to ensure that the apt lists are retained during the playwright installation process. By moving the cleanup step after the installation, it prevents errors related to missing package lists, thereby enhancing the build process without impacting app deployment. This change is part of ongoing improvements to the deployment workflow.

items.title: Update connector icons for global accessibility in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update re-hosts all connector icons on the CDN to ensure they load properly across different regions. Previously, some icons were blocked or failed to render due to cross-origin restrictions. Now, all 11 icons are accessible regardless of the user's location, enhancing the user experience.

items.title: Enhance AskUserQuestion Feedback in Coding Bridge Agent 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the handling of user responses in the AskUserQuestion feature. It ensures that structured answers are correctly passed through the system, allowing the CLI to acknowledge user input instead of indicating that no response was given. New tests have been added to verify this functionality, enhancing overall reliability.

items.title: Enhance Cookie-Capture Extension with Auth Match in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `auth.acedata.cloud` content-script match for the cookie-capture extension, allowing it to inject on the connectors page. The version is bumped to v0.3, and TypeScript build issues have been resolved, ensuring a clean repackage. The installation documentation has also been updated to reflect these changes.

items.title: Update acedataext Install Docs for v0.3 Package in Backend 🚀
items.summary: The installation documentation for the acedataext browser extension has been updated to point to the new v0.3 package. This change affects all 18 locales, ensuring users download the correct file that includes the necessary content-script match for auth.acedata.cloud. The live documentation will reflect this update after the merge.

items.title: Fix AskUserQuestion Answer Handling in Coding Bridge Agent 🤖
items.summary: This update resolves an issue where answers to AskUserQuestion prompts were not being processed correctly, leading to misleading messages. Key changes include plumbing the answer through the system and updating the PermissionBroker to handle answers appropriately. New tests have been added to ensure functionality and verify that the CLI now correctly echoes user responses.
